anomalies.title: Spec NEW_STR vs expected line count off-by-one
anomalies.description: Spec post-paste check expects 1922 lines but explicit NEW_STR rendering produces 1921 (24 NEW_STR lines minus 3 OLD_STR lines = 21 net new lines; 1900 + 21 = 1921). Spec structure summary '1 blank + ===== + header + ===== + 1 blank + 14-line body + 1 blank + closing =====' sums to 21, not 22 as headlined. Body is also 13 lines (not 14): [OQ-...] header + 12 prose lines.
anomalies.remediation_options: (a) operator accepts 1921-line state and amends spec / (b) manual single-line append to reach 1922 / (c) full revert + re-fire with corrected spec

anomalies.title: Spec OLD_STR L1899 anchor mismatch (2 spaces vs bare empty)
anomalies.description: Spec OLD_STR + 'Note' both claim L1899 has 2 trailing spaces ('  '). Byte-level inspection of pre-paste CMB.txt showed L1899 is a bare empty line (only \n; bytes 66 0A 0A 3D). STEP 1 anchor checks only L1898 and L1900, not L1899, so the mismatch was not surfaced by the spec's STEP 1 procedure. If executer had used spec's OLD_STR verbatim, edit-tool would have failed with 'old_str not found'.
anomalies.severity: non-blocking (executer corrected OLD_STR to actual content; documented as judgment call J1)
anomalies.remediation: Future similar prompts should add an explicit L1899 anchor to STEP 1, OR use byte-level pre-fire verification of the full OLD_STR rather than just sentinel lines.

anomalies.title: Spec body line count claimed as 14, actual is 13
anomalies.description: Spec says 'NEW_STR... 14-line body'. Counting: [OQ-2026-05-06-048R-EARLY-FIRE] (1) + 12 prose lines (048R W19-closing... through 'T1 Synth W20 weekly: select OPT_*.') = 13 lines. Source-of-truth substrate file siarc-relay-bridge/sessions/2026-05-06/048R-EARLY-FIRE-DECISION-SUBSTRATE/cmb_open_question_entry.txt has 13 lines (verified via Get-Content count). Possibly contributes to the off-by-one in D1.

anomalies.title: Spec STEP 3 'recorded post-bytes' band 85549 +- paste-bytes vs actual 87292
anomalies.description: Spec AEAL-A2 entry says 'post-paste SHA-256 of CMB.txt = <recorded>, 1922 lines, ~85549 bytes ± paste-bytes'. Actual post-bytes = 87292 (delta +1743). The '~85549 ± paste-bytes' phrasing is loose and 87292 is consistent with adding ~1700 bytes of new content, but the spec didn't precompute a tight expected range.
